title: Report projects U.S. population decline as birth rates remain low | Institute for Family Studies
gist: An EWTN News writeup of the IFS 'Demographic Dead End' report says the US is approaching a demographic turning point, with fertility well below replacement and population decline increasingly likely in coming decades absent change.

insights.claim: IFS warns the US is approaching a demographic turning point, with fertility rates continuing to fall well below replacement and population declines increasingly likely in coming decades unless trends change.
insights.speaker: Institute for Family Studies
insights.so_what: Below-replacement fertility becoming a durable, mainstream-covered outlook underpins long-run bearishness on US labor supply and domestic demand.
insights.locator: A report from the Institute for Family Studies

insights.claim: The report presents state-level fertility estimates back to 1917 and reconstructs Massachusetts birth rates to 1660, offering one of the longest historical views of American fertility ever compiled.
insights.speaker: Institute for Family Studies
insights.so_what: A uniquely long dataset lends credibility to the claim that today's decline is historically unprecedented in depth and breadth.
